title: China Financial Threat Mitigation Act of 2025
short_title: China Financial Threat Mitigation Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law tells the Treasury Secretary to study how much America relies on China's money and banking system. The Secretary needs to figure out what problems this could cause for the United States. Within one year, the Secretary must write a report explaining what they found and what America should do about it. The report will be given to Congress and put online for everyone to read.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the China Financial Threat Mitigation Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Study and Report on Financial Threat Mitigation with Respect to People's Republic of China
sections.text:
Within one year after this law is passed, the Secretary of the Treasury must work with several other government officials to study and write a report about how much the United States depends on China's financial system. The Treasury Secretary must work together with the head of the Federal Reserve (the central bank), the head of the Securities and Exchange Commission (which watches over stock markets), the head of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (which watches over trading in things like oil and grain), and the Secretary of State (who handles foreign relations).

The report must include four main parts. First, it must look at how problems in China's financial system could hurt the United States and the world's financial systems. Second, it must describe what the United States government is already doing to protect America's financial stability from these risks. Third, it must describe and judge how honest, complete, and trustworthy the economic information that comes from China really is. Fourth, it must give suggestions for what else the United States government should do, including how American representatives at international organizations should work with other countries to watch for and reduce financial risks from China.

The Treasury Secretary must send this report to four congressional committees within one year. These are the Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations, the House Committee on Financial Services, and the House Committee on Foreign Affairs. The Secretary must also send the report to American representatives at relevant international organizations when appropriate.

The report must not contain classified or secret information, but it can have a separate classified section attached to it. Within one year of this law being passed, the Treasury Secretary must publish the report on the Treasury Department's website. However, any classified section will not be published online.
